articles.title: IMF expects world economy to grow a sluggish 3% this year, weighed down by Iran war but helped by AI
articles.description: The International Monetary Fund on Wednesday modestly downgraded its outlook for the world economy this year, citing the energy shock caused by the Iran war. But the fallout from the conflict is being partially offset by booming investment in artificial intelligence and other technologies. The IMF now expects the global economy to expand by a sluggish 3% in 2026, down from 3.5% last year and from the 3.1% it had forecast for this year back in April. The war pushed up energy prices, squeezing businesses and consumers. The IMF now expects oil prices to be up nearly 32% this year and for overall global inflation to accelerate to 4.7% in 2026.

articles.title: Dollar stands tall as Gulf tensions fuel oil price surge, Fed hike bets
articles.description: The U.S. dollar held firm against major currencies on Thursday. Renewed Gulf tensions revived safe-haven bids, boosting the dollar's strength. Surging oil prices also fueled rate hike bets, keeping the yen under pressure. The Japanese yen struggled near a 40-year trough against the dollar. U.S. Treasury yields rose as markets priced in higher rate hike risks.

articles.title: Oil rises after US launches fresh strikes against Iran
articles.description: Oil prices increased on Thursday following new U.S. strikes against Iran. These actions have diminished hopes for peace and the Strait of Hormuz reopening. Brent and West Texas Intermediate crude futures saw notable gains in early trading. Shipowners are expected to adopt a more cautious approach to voyages through the strait. Insurance providers are reviewing policy terms after recent vessel attacks.

articles.title: Asian shares climb on chip rally, oil jumps as Gulf hostilities resume
articles.description: Asian shares climbed on Thursday as semiconductors got a respite from heavy selling, though ​gains were capped by a surge in oil prices as a resumption of hostilities in the Gulf reignited ‌inflation fears and hammered bonds.

articles.title: Asian Markets Rally Amidst Inflation and Oil Price Surges
articles.description: Asian shares saw gains on Thursday as semiconductors experienced a rally following heavy sell-offs. However, rising oil prices due to renewed Gulf conflict raised inflation concerns. Wall Street showed mixed reactions, while global bond markets felt the pressure. The situation remains unstable, affecting global economies and trade.

articles.title: Crude oil prices extend gains on renewed US-Iran war tensions; Brent climbs above $78 per barrel
articles.description: US-Iran war: Brent crude futures were up 78 cents, or 1%, at $78.80 a barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude gained 74 cents, or 1.01%, to trade at $74.26 a barrel.
